title: Excel Column Title
hints: This is similar to converting a number to base-26, but with a twist: it's 1-indexed instead of 0-indexed.
Before taking modulo, subtract 1 from A to convert from 1-indexed to 0-indexed.
The remainder gives you the current character (0-25 maps to A-Z).
After getting the character, divide A by 26 and continue.
Build the result from right to left, then reverse it at the end.
For example: 27 -> subtract 1 to get 26 -> 26 % 26 = 0 (A), 26 / 26 = 1 -> subtract 1 to get 0 -> 0 % 26 = 0 (A) -> result is 'AA'.
